message:
feat(persist+runtime): RDB hash-with-field-TTLs encoding + roundtrip (br-frankenredis-th7q, rv89 part 4e)

Closes the RDB persistence gap for hashes that carry per-field TTLs.
Without this, a SAVE + shutdown + reload would emit plain RDB_TYPE_HASH
and lose the field_expires map.

On-disk format (FrankenRedis-private — upstream interop lives under
frankenredis-v8s5 / frankenredis-s5su):

  [u8 type=21][key:string][u32 len]
    (field:string, value:string, expires_ms:u64) × len

The u64 uses the sentinel u64::MAX for "field has no TTL" so a hash
can carry a mix of TTL'd and non-TTL'd fields in one record. 21 is
intentionally the same numeric value as upstream's RDB_TYPE_HASH_METADATA
for future alignment.

Wiring:
- fr-persist: new RdbValue::HashWithTtls variant; encode_rdb emits
  type 21; decode_rdb accepts type 21 and reconstructs the vector.
- fr-runtime::store_to_rdb_entries probes hash_field_expires and
  emits HashWithTtls only when at least one field has a TTL; plain
  hashes stay on RDB_TYPE_HASH (4) so older RDB readers continue
  working unchanged.
- fr-runtime::apply_rdb_entries_to_store HSETs every field, then
  seeds hash_field_expires directly to reinstate each deadline.

Tests added (2 fr-runtime lib tests):
  - rdb_roundtrip_preserves_hash_field_ttls_via_hashwithttls_variant
  - rdb_roundtrip_hash_without_ttls_keeps_plain_type_tag

fr-runtime 274/0; fr-persist 95/0; workspace clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: cc-redis <noreply@anthropic.com>
